aggregate.note: Compiled through database admin routing and exposed by both database.command(...) and the local wire passthrough.

buildInfo.note: Static local metadata sourced from the dialect/runtime descriptor.

collStats.note: Served from local collection stats snapshots.

connectionStatus.note: Static local auth/runtime shape, with wire auth info patched from the active connection.

count.note: Compiled through the same find-selection path used by direct count command routing.

create.note: Namespace administration routed through database admin services.

createIndexes.note: Index administration routed through local collection/index services, including local-only per-index metadata such as hidden indexes.

currentOp.note: Exposes the local active-operation registry for embedded-runtime API and wire parity; no distributed semantics are attempted.

dbHash.note: Computes stable local collection hashes for embedded-runtime verification across documents, indexes and collection options.

dbStats.note: Served from local database stats snapshots.

delete.note: Write command orchestration routes each delete spec through the same write semantics as the public API.

distinct.note: Compiled through the same read-selection path used by direct distinct execution.

drop.note: Namespace administration routed through database admin services.

dropDatabase.note: Implemented as local namespace lifecycle cleanup plus runtime invalidation.

dropIndexes.note: Index administration routed through local collection/index services.

explain.note: Delegates to routed find/aggregate/update/delete/count/distinct/findAndModify explain builders and preserves the explained command shape.

find.note: Compiled through the same find operation path as the public collection surface.

findAndModify.note: Routes through explicit find-and-modify orchestration shared by database.command(...) and wire passthrough.

insert.note: Write command orchestration routes each insert batch through the same collection semantics as the public API.

isMaster.note: Legacy handshake alias sharing the local hello metadata source of truth.

ismaster.note: Legacy handshake alias sharing the local hello metadata source of truth.

listCollections.note: Namespace listing routed through local snapshots with filter/nameOnly support.

listCommands.note: Static inventory of the supported raw command surface, including admin family, wire availability and explain availability metadata.

listDatabases.note: Database listing routed through local snapshots with filter/nameOnly support.

listIndexes.note: Index metadata surfaced through local collection/index services.

killOp.note: Best-effort local cancellation over operations registered as killable in the embedded runtime; no remote or cluster cancellation exists.

profile.note: Local profiling control and introspection routed through engine profiling support, including current level and recorded entry counts.

renameCollection.note: Namespace administration routed through database admin services.

serverStatus.note: Static local runtime metadata enriched with engine information, embedded-runtime counters and profiling summary.

update.note: Write command orchestration routes each update spec through the same write semantics as the public API.

validate.note: Validation snapshots routed through local collection validation support.

hint.note: Validated against existing indexes and applied to read planning/explain where engines can honor it.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata and surfaced by explain().

max_time_ms.note: Enforced as a local deadline during read execution and explain().

batch_size.note: Async and sync find cursors now fetch local batches before yielding results, even though engines remain in-process.

hint.note: Applied through the underlying find() path used to count matching documents.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying read path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Enforced through the underlying find() path used to count documents.

hint.note: Applied through the underlying find() path used to enumerate distinct values.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying read path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Enforced through the underlying find() path used to enumerate distinct values.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying full-collection read path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Enforced through the underlying full-collection read path.

hint.note: Applied through the pushdown find() path used by aggregate() and surfaced in explain().

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata and propagated through aggregate explain/materialization.

max_time_ms.note: Applied to referenced collection loads, pushdown reads and final pipeline materialization.

batch_size.note: Positive batch sizes trigger chunked execution for streamable aggregate pipelines; global stages still fall back to full materialization.

allow_disk_use.note: Controls whether the aggregation cursor may use the configured spill-to-disk policy for blocking stages.

let.note: Propagated into aggregate expression evaluation and subpipelines.

array_filters.note: Applied during update execution for supported filtered positional paths.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection before single-document update execution.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

sort.note: Implemented with profile-aware validation since PyMongo 4.11.

array_filters.note: Applied during per-document update execution for supported filtered positional paths.

hint.note: Applied through hinted _id preselection before per-document updates.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection before replacement.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

sort.note: Implemented with profile-aware validation since PyMongo 4.11.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection before delete.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

hint.note: Applied through hinted _id preselection before per-document deletes.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

array_filters.note: Propagated to the underlying update_one() execution for supported filtered positional paths.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection and post-update fetch.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and enforced there.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

sort.note: Implemented through update_one()/find semantics with profile-aware validation.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection and post-replacement fetch.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and enforced there.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

sort.note: Implemented through replace_one()/find semantics with profile-aware validation.
Implemented through find() selection semantics before delete.

hint.note: Applied through hinted document selection before delete.

comment.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and session metadata.

max_time_ms.note: Propagated through the underlying read selection path and enforced there.

let.note: Command-level let variables are available through $expr in write filters and selection paths.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the batch write operation.

let.note: Command-level let variables flow into per-operation write filters through $expr when requests do not override them.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.
Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

max_time_ms.note: Enforced as a local deadline during index build and multikey backfill.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

max_time_ms.note: Enforced as a local deadline across the whole index batch.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.
Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

hint.note: Propagated through command routing into aggregate pushdown/explain.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata and surfaced through explain/materialization.

maxTimeMS.note: Enforced against local aggregate execution and explain paths.

allowDiskUse.note: Applied to blocking aggregate stages through the same local spill policy as the public API.

let.note: Propagated into aggregate expression evaluation and subpipelines.

batchSize.note: Materialized into the command cursor surface for streamable pipelines.

scale.note: Applied to size-oriented metrics in the local collection stats snapshot.

showPrivileges.note: Controls whether the local auth status document includes the privileges array.

query.note: Compiled into the same local find-selection path used to execute the count command.

skip.note: Applied before materializing the local count result.

limit.note: Applied before materializing the local count result.

hint.note: Applied through the compiled selection path used by the count command.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the command execution.

maxTimeMS.note: Enforced through the compiled selection path used by the count command.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

maxTimeMS.note: Enforced as a local deadline across the command index batch.

comment.note: Accepted for command parity and recorded in local admin profiling metadata when supplied.

collections.note: Limits hashing to the selected local collections while preserving a stable collection-order contract.

comment.note: Accepted for command parity and recorded in command profiling metadata.

scale.note: Applied to size-oriented metrics in the local database stats snapshot.

ordered.note: Controls short-circuiting when one delete specification fails.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write command.

let.note: Propagated into per-delete filters through $expr when supplied on individual specs.

query.note: Compiled into the same local selection path used to enumerate distinct values.

hint.note: Applied through the underlying read selection path used by the command.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the command execution.

maxTimeMS.note: Enforced through the underlying distinct selection path.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

verbosity.note: Accepted and surfaced in explain responses for supported routed commands, including find/aggregate/update/delete/count/distinct/findAndModify.

comment.note: Propagated into the explained command where that command supports comment.

maxTimeMS.note: Propagated into the explained command where that command supports maxTimeMS.

filter.note: Compiled into the same local find operation shape as the collection API.

projection.note: Applied to command cursor materialization using the same projection semantics as the collection API.

sort.note: Applied to command read planning and result materialization.

hint.note: Applied to command read planning and surfaced in explain.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the command execution.

maxTimeMS.note: Enforced during command read execution and explain.

batchSize.note: Materialized into the command cursor surface.

arrayFilters.note: Applied through the underlying write path for supported filtered positional updates.

hint.note: Applied through hinted selection and post-write fetch.

maxTimeMS.note: Propagated through the underlying selection path and enforced there.

let.note: Propagated into command-level $expr evaluation for the write filter.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the command execution.

bypassDocumentValidation.note: Validated and propagated through replacement/update command routing.

sort.note: Applied through the underlying find-and-modify selection path.

ordered.note: Controls short-circuiting when one insert document fails.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write command.

bypassDocumentValidation.note: Validated and propagated through command routing.

filter.note: Applied to the local namespace snapshot before cursor materialization.

nameOnly.note: Controls the fields exposed by the listCollections cursor.

authorizedCollections.note: Accepted for wire/API parity and preserved in the normalized command options.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the admin read command.

filter.note: Applied to the local database snapshot before materialization.

nameOnly.note: Controls the fields exposed by the listDatabases response.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the admin read command.
Recorded in engine session metadata for index administration.

op.note: Identifies the locally registered operation to cancel on a best-effort basis.

comment.note: Accepted for command parity and recorded in local admin profiling metadata when supplied.

slowms.note: Propagated through the local profiling control path when a profiling level update is requested; status queries also surface current level and entry counts.

ordered.note: Controls short-circuiting when one update specification fails.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the write command.

let.note: Propagated into per-update filters through $expr when supplied on individual specs.

bypassDocumentValidation.note: Validated and propagated through replacement/update command routing.

arrayFilters.note: Applied through per-update write execution for supported filtered positional paths.

scandata.note: Controls whether storage-engine level scan metadata is requested in the validation snapshot.

full.note: Controls whether the validation snapshot requests the expanded pass.

background.note: Validated and surfaced in the validation snapshot contract.

comment.note: Recorded in engine session metadata for the validation command.

mongodb-core.name: mongodb-core
mongodb-core.description: Reusable minimum profile for basic MongoDB tests and resources.

mongodb-text-search.name: mongodb-text-search
mongodb-text-search.description: Reusable profile for tests and resources that need textual $search without requiring vector search.

mongodb-search.description: Reusable profile for tests and resources with search and vector search.

mongodb-platform.description: Reusable profile for MongoDB runtimes with platform surface.

operations.name: estimated_document_count
operations.description: Return a fast estimated document count.

operations.name: distinct
operations.description: Return distinct values for a path.

write.description: Insert, update, and delete document operations.

search.description: Support for Atlas Search-style textual queries.

operations.name: aggregate
operations.description: Run aggregate pipelines that start with $search.

metadata.note: The local textual $search tier is closed in its documented subset; remaining gaps are advanced Atlas-like features.

operations.name: aggregate
operations.description: Run aggregate pipelines that start with $vectorSearch.

metadata.note: SQLiteEngine uses a local usearch ANN backend when the vector index is materialized; MemoryEngine remains the exact semantic baseline.

classicText.note: The classic $text subset remains local, token-based and intentionally lighter than MongoDB server full-text behavior.

geospatial.note: The embedded runtime uses planar local geometry operations. $nearSphere and 2dsphere remain Mongo-like names over local planar distance.

vectorSearch.note: SQLiteEngine uses a local usearch ANN backend when the vector index is materialized; MemoryEngine remains the exact semantic baseline.

search.note: The local textual $search tier is closed in its documented subset; remaining gaps are advanced Atlas-like features.

classicText.note: The classic $text subset remains local, token-based and intentionally lighter than MongoDB server full-text behavior.

geospatial.note: The embedded runtime uses planar local geometry operations. $nearSphere and 2dsphere remain Mongo-like names over local planar distance.
